title: Glomera rigidula
taxonomy: Glomera rigidula is classified within the Orchidaceae family, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. This orchid family consists of approximately 30,000 known species and is divided into several subfamilies and tribes. Glomera rigidula, in particular, is part of the Epidendroideae subfamily, which includes the majority of orchid species. Further classification of Glomera rigidula would involve its tribe and genus, but specific details are not readily available. More research is necessary to provide a complete and accurate classification of this species.

intro: Glomera rigidula, also known as the "Borneo orchid," is a type of flowering orchid native to the tropical regions of Borneo. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Glomera rigidula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Glomera rigidula is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is commonly used in the form of a cough syrup or expectorant to help relieve respiratory congestion.
